# ShelterPulse

> Simulation and optimization lab for cat-shelter resource allocation under uncertainty.

## What it does

ShelterPulse uses discrete-event simulation (SimPy) and Bayesian optimization to find
the best budget allocation for cat shelters during kitten season. It minimizes overflow
cat-days across four interventions: foster support, extra clinic hours, temporary
isolation capacity, and adoption events.

## Key results

- Whisker Haven demo: BO reduces overflow from 234 to 0 cat-days
- 20 candidates x 32 replications in < 30 seconds
- 5 named baseline strategies compared per sweep
- Common Random Numbers ensure fair comparison between candidates

## Architecture

- **Simulation engine**: SimPy discrete-event model of complete cat lifecycle
- **Optimizer**: Gaussian Process + Expected Improvement (jaxbo primary, scipy fallback)
- **API**: FastAPI REST (Python 3.12)
- **Frontend**: Next.js 16 + React + TypeScript + Tailwind CSS (static export)
- **Deployment**: nginx + uvicorn in one ECS Fargate container

## API

Base URL: /api

| Method | Endpoint      | Description                                    |
|--------|---------------|------------------------------------------------|
| GET    | /health       | Health check                                   |
| POST   | /simulate     | Run simulation with a given budget allocation  |
| POST   | /optimize     | Run Bayesian optimization sweep                |
| GET    | /baselines    | Get named baseline strategies                  |
| POST   | /export       | Download full results as ZIP                   |
| GET    | /docs         | OpenAPI interactive documentation              |

## Pages

| Path             | Description                                  |
|------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| /en              | Landing page (English)                       |
| /es              | Landing page (Spanish)                       |
| /en/demo         | Interactive demo with Whisker Haven scenario |
| /en/how-it-works | Technical explainer of the simulation        |
| /en/simulate     | Custom scenario builder                      |

## Source code
